paper_title: Stochastic models of compliant spine arrays for rough surface grasping
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/09_Stochastic models of compliant spine arrays for rough surface grasping.pdf
sections_checked: Appendix A. Derivation of a micro-spine's travel following an exponential distribution
Appendix B. Justification of sample size selection in asperity distribution characterizations
Appendix C. Single-tile two-dimensional adhesion modeling details
Appendix D. Design parameter tuning for three surface properties
Appendix E. Opposed spine mechanism two-dimensional adhesion modeling

equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1. Fn/Fs = hmis/dunit = tan(theta_m) was visually checked together with the toe-heel geometry and the Fc -> 0 limiting condition.

Retained as E2 exactly as printed: alpha*lambda*exp(-lambda*x) + (1-alpha)*delta(x), x > 0. The prose clearly assigns a point mass at x=0, so downstream use must treat this as a mixed distribution on x>=0 rather than an ordinary density only on x>0.

Piecewise empirical survival probability was visually verified. Omitted as a displayed equation to remain within the eight-formula limit; the 0.5 N lower cutoff, 3 N censoring point and eta tails are retained in the parameter table.
Retained as E3. The integral limits, p(alpha,lambda;x), F(x), Pr(F(x)) and footprint denominator were checked against the rendered page.
Retained as E3. Linear flexure force and K=Fmax/dstroke relation were checked.
Retained as E4. Opposed shear components subtract and normal components add.
Both left and right vector-magnitude limits were verified; preserved textually as the absolute-force constraint accompanying Eq. (6).
Retained as E5. Both arctan(Fn/Fs) < theta_th inequalities were visually checked.

Appendix A memoryless derivation was checked. It only derives that random-start travel to the next asperity has the same exponential law and is not needed once Eq. (2) and its assumption are retained.

Single-tile shear-normal integral, geometry, angle and displacement-limit equations were visually checked. They are indexed in the route card but omitted from the compact card because Eq. (4)-(5), the stated re-engagement factor, Fig. 12 and full source location are sufficient for this extraction.
Retained as E6. Statistical tile preload equals middle-spring force Km*Lm+Finit.
Left/right middle-spring shear forces were checked; omitted because Eq. (19), Eq. (26) and the route location preserve the required interface with less context.
Left/right tile shear-normal integrals were checked, including the re-engagement term on the left tile. They repeat the single-tile structure with side-specific variables.
Retained as E7. Full opposed-array external-force composition matches Eq. (6).
Ltotal = L + Lm verified and stated under E6.
Preload and side-specific spine force-length relations were visually checked and indexed for full-model reuse.

The PDF clearly prints tan(phi_1'(z)) = H/(D1+Lo-x). This conflicts with the z argument and with Eq. (17), which uses Lo+D-z. The intended variable cannot be corrected from the paper alone; do not implement Eq. (29) silently.
Full-stroke condition Dm=Dlimit or H=Hlimit was checked.

figures_checked.reason: Application photographs are not needed for the mechanism card.
Toe-heel geometry and safe-angle shrinkage are adequately preserved by Eq. (1) and text.
The fitted alpha/lambda values and the three surface conditions are retained numerically.
The 0.5-3 N censoring range and eta tails are retained; no exact values were estimated from the curves.
Tile footprint, stroke, hard stop and flexure variables are captured by Eq. (4)-(5).
One-pass decision tree is replaced by the more complete recursive MDP figure.
Validation trend and trial counts are retained textually; precise curve values were not read from the graph.
Irreplaceable state-transition structure for hold, slip, no engagement and re-engagement; 300-dpi crop visually verified.
The weak-surface improvement and recursion-cost trend are preserved textually.
The high-density/weak-asperity error boundary is preserved qualitatively without reading grid values.
Preload/load trajectory and displacement limits are represented by the retained equations and parameters.
Minimal single-tile model-experiment evidence including mean, standard deviation and constraint transitions; 300-dpi crop visually verified.
Unit-length tradeoff is retained as a conditional trend; graph points are not treated as transferable optima.
Stroke-density tradeoff is preserved in the mechanism and parameter text.
Maximum-force/asperity-strength trend is preserved textually.
Opposed mechanism states are sufficiently defined by Fig. 17, equations and text.
Irreplaceable opposed-spine geometry and preload-dependent safe regions; 300-dpi crop visually verified.
Opposed model validation is retained textually with trial count, binning and mechanism parameters.
Preload normal-versus-shear tradeoff is compactly preserved as a mechanism conclusion.
Preferred stiff/soft-plus-bias spring cases are retained numerically.
Perching-gripper hardware details are not needed beyond retained parameters.
Flight sequence is an application demonstration and is summarized by success rate.
Random-start exponential memoryless result is stated textually.
Spatial-parameter convergence justifies 150 trials and is summarized textually.
Strength-distribution convergence is summarized textually.
Heatmap supports the retained trend that optimal stroke is controlled mainly by spatial parameters.
Heatmap supports the retained trend that optimal Fmax is controlled mainly by strength distribution.

omitted_sections: General robot-application history in the Introduction
Full reference list and administrative material
Detailed quadrotor truss/tendon implementation beyond mechanism-relevant parameters
Long Appendix A algebra after verifying the memoryless result

uncertain_items.item: Variable mismatch in opposed-tile re-engagement angle
uncertain_items.detail: phi_1' is written as a function of z but the denominator ends in -x. Eq. (17) and the corresponding force Eq. (28) use z. The intended correction requires author code or re-derivation.
The printed condition x>0 accompanies a delta mass at x=0. The prose makes the point mass explicit; implement the model as a mixed measure on x>=0.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.8 and p.16, Section 3.2 and Appendix C
uncertain_items.detail: The linear factor based on average asperity height/depth and the tile-level angle limit are empirical closures, not geometry-resolved three-dimensional contact laws.

known_warnings: The simplified and recursive array models assume independent spines and explicitly omit dynamic load redistribution after a spine fails.
The authors state that adding redistribution should increase failure probability, reduce total capacity and increase variation.
Spatial and strength distributions are assumed independent; opposed validation additionally assumes isotropic and nearly identical sides.
Contact strengths below 0.5 N are neglected and measurements above 3 N are right-censored into eta.
The tile does not support lateral y loading; row-engagement moments are ignored in the opposed mechanism model.
All equations, parameters, captions and retained crops were checked against 180-dpi full-page and 300-dpi selected-page renders.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains independent surface, single-tile, recursive and opposed-array submodels.
